panels.title: Platform components UP
panels.description: JSON poller ← /health/platform. ANY component not green => red (§6.2 row0).

panels.title: DHCP achieved/offered DORA
panels.description: textfile. <90% sustained => we found a ceiling / load-gen sat (§6.2/§6.5).

panels.title: DNS achieved/offered QPS
panels.description: textfile. <90% sustained => ceiling (§6.2/§6.5).

panels.title: lease→DNS propagation p95
panels.description: JSON ← orchestrator. >12s = SLO breach (§6.2 row0).

panels.title: PG conns vs max
panels.description: postgres_exporter. >70% amber / >85% red (§6.2/§6.5 BACK OFF at 85%).

panels.title: PG cache hit ratio
panels.description: postgres_exporter pg_stat_database. <95% amber / <90% red (§6.2/§6.5).

panels.title: Pod restarts (1h)
panels.description: kube-state-metrics. ANY restart = red (§6.2 row0 / §6.5 soak fail).

panels.title: Redis used vs maxmemory
panels.description: redis_exporter. >80% amber; ANY evicted_keys = red (see row3) (§6.2 row0).

panels.title: DHCP offered vs achieved DORA/s
panels.description: textfile. achieved diverging BELOW offered = load-gen sat OR appliance ceiling (§6.2 row1).

panels.title: DHCP ACK latency p50/p95/p99
panels.description: textfile. p99 breaching dhcp_ack_p99_ms SLO (default 50ms) / tail blowout (§6.2 row1).

panels.title: DHCP drops / orphans / NAKs
panels.description: textfile. DECLINE+TIMEOUT are the protocol-tier health signal for criterion (c) (§3.5/§6.2).

panels.title: DNS offered vs achieved QPS
panels.description: textfile. achieved below offered = ceiling / load-gen sat (§6.2 row1).

panels.title: DNS resolve latency p50/p95/p99
panels.description: textfile. p99 > dns_resolve_p99_ms SLO (default 20ms) (§6.2 row1).

panels.title: Operator API-mutation latency + 5xx
panels.description: JSON ← orchestrator. 5xx>0, latency rising with concurrency = audit-lock contention (§6.2 row1, criterion a).

panels.title: Kea DORA funnel (discover→ack)
panels.description: JSON ← /metrics/dhcp/timeseries (60s). discover >> ack = dropping / pool empty (§6.2 row2).

panels.title: Kea NAK rate
panels.description: JSON. Sustained NAK = pool exhaustion / config (§6.2 row2). Pool util% lives in the §6.5 watch — raise pool, not a real ceiling.

panels.title: BIND qps + RCODE mix
panels.description: JSON ← /metrics/dns/timeseries (60s). qps plateau below offered; SERVFAIL climbing = starved (§6.2 row2).

panels.title: BIND SERVFAIL rate (isolated)
panels.description: JSON. SERVFAIL climbing = BIND starved (CPU contention, §6.2 row2 / Layer5 attribution).

panels.title: BIND RRL drops / slips
panels.description: textfile RCODE. REFUSED MUST be 0 (§4.8/§6.1) — any REFUSED = out-of-zone query bug, not an intended miss. RRL drops/slips (dns_metric_sample.rate_dropped/slipped) surface via the JSON poller dns series when populated.

panels.title: DNS NXDOMAIN rate (in-zone misses)
panels.description: JSON + textfile. The deliberate ~2% in-zone miss slice (§1.7). Authoritative NXDOMAIN is EXPECTED; REFUSED is not (see left panel).

panels.title: lease-events POST p50/p95/p99
panels.description: textfile/structlog. THE leading DB-write-pressure indicator. p95 > 1s = headline DB ceiling — STOP RAMPING (§6.2 row3 / §6.5).

panels.title: Celery queue depth (LLEN)
panels.description: redis_exporter --check-keys. ANY monotonic climb > 5min = criterion (d) FAIL (§6.2 row3 / §6.5).

panels.title: Redis ops + evicted_keys
panels.description: redis_exporter. ANY evicted_keys > 0 = BACK OFF (§6.2 row3 / §6.5). maxmemory 256mb (§2.1).

panels.title: Redis wake-bus subscribers + publishes/s
panels.description: JSON ← /admin/redis/wake-bus. Surge spike in dns_group publishes = DDNS wake-storm (§4.6/§6.2 row3).

panels.title: Celery sweep duration
panels.description: Approaching the 300s cadence = overlap / lock storm. >240s (80%) = BACK OFF (§6.2 row3 / §6.5).

panels.title: D1 · conns by state
panels.description: postgres_exporter. idle-in-transaction climbing = the real pool-killer (§6.2 D1 / §6.5 BACK OFF >20 climbing).

panels.title: D2 · active conns vs 200 + app-pool line
panels.description: postgres_exporter. Annotate the inferred app-pool ceiling at 30×api-replicas (§2.1 pool 30/process). Plateau at the pool line = pool saturation (§6.2 D2).

panels.title: D3 · cache-hit + temp spill
panels.description: postgres_exporter pg_stat_database. temp_bytes>0 = work_mem(16MB) spill on big SELECTs (§2.1/§6.2 D3).

panels.title: D4 · per-table dead-tuple ratio
panels.description: postgres_exporter custom. Ramp WITHOUT reset across the overnight trough = autovacuum losing (§6.2 D4 / §1.4 / §6.5 SOAK). Focus tables only.

panels.title: D4b · last-autovacuum age per table
panels.description: postgres_exporter custom. -1 = never vacuumed. Age climbing unboundedly on a hot table = autovacuum not keeping up (§6.2 D4).

panels.title: D5 · focus-table size growth
panels.description: postgres_exporter custom. dns_record_op is MONOTONIC (no prune today — §5.5 H). dhcp_lease_history / dns_query_log_entry on a disk-full slope = soak fail (§6.2 D5 / §6.5).

panels.title: D6 · lock waits + deadlocks/s
panels.description: postgres_exporter custom (pg_locks + pg_stat_database). ANY deadlock = criterion-(a) FAIL; sustained lock-wait > 0 (esp. dns_zone H3) = contention (§6.2 D6 / §6.5).

panels.title: D8 · WAL rate
panels.description: postgres_exporter custom. WAL generation rate (§6.2 D8). Sustained high WAL = write amplification.

panels.title: D9 · top slow queries (pg_stat_statements)
panels.description: postgres_exporter custom. The per-zone DNSRecord N+1 / sweep subnet-scan rising to the top = the §5.3 hypotheses firing (§6.2 D9). Requires pg_stat_statements pre-loaded (§5.4).

panels.title: D10 · longest txn age
panels.description: postgres_exporter custom. A long-held txn pins autovacuum's xmin horizon = bloat (§6.2 D10).

panels.title: node CPU per-mode + load
panels.description: node-exporter. Pinned cores => 'DB slow' may actually be bind9/kea CPU starvation (§2.1/§6.2 row5 — the attribution panel).

panels.title: node disk %util + await (CNPG PV)
panels.description: node-exporter. %util pegged 100% > 30s on the CNPG PV device = disk bottleneck, NOT a lock wait (§6.2 row5 / §6.5 BACK OFF).

panels.title: node mem + swap
panels.description: node-exporter. swap-in > 0 = overcommit (§6.2 row5). bind9/kea uncapped can drive this (§2.1).

panels.title: per-pod CPU (cAdvisor)
panels.description: cAdvisor via kubelet. postgres vs bind9 vs kea — a surge that pins bind9/kea can starve Postgres and look like a DB stall (§5.4/§6.2 row5).

panels.title: per-pod working-set vs limit (cAdvisor)
panels.description: cAdvisor. CNPG working-set approaching 1Gi limit (§2.1). working-set > 90% of limit = BACK OFF (§6.5).

panels.title: OOMKilled (1h) + node disk free
panels.description: KSM + node-exporter. ANY OOMKilled = soak fail. Disk-free projection crossing zero before run end = BACK OFF (§6.2 row5 / §6.5).
